entry.title: SAP GTS QUESTION

entry.title: Individual purchase requisitons from a single sales order
entry.content:
Hello,

I am working on a procure-to-order scenario (individual purchase order) with item category group: BANC, item category: TAB, and schedule line category: CB. When I save the sales order, the system generates a single purchase requisition containing multiple line items corresponding to the sales order lines.

What is the configuration tbd so the system, will create different PRs when saving the sales order.

entry.title: Serial number already locked by the user in Production order
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

When generating serial numbers using the newly created serial number in the production order, I encounter an error stating that the serial number is already locked by a user. However, upon checking in SM12, there are no lock entries for any user.

Regardless of the quantity of serials being generated, the system continues to display this error. Could you please advise on how to resolve this issue?

entry.title: Problems with USING ALL CLIENTS
entry.content:
Dear SAP-Community

I have an abap-sql, that selects data on two clients (100 and 700). Some data is in client 100 and some other in client 700.
With the old known CLIENT SPECIFIED a SELECT with CLIENT is possible. 

The following code functions, but returns a warning saying I should use USING ALL CLIENTS instead of client specified:


lv_mandt_100 = '100'.
lv_mandt_700 = '700'.

SELECT
FROM ekko AS ekko_100
LEFT OUTER JOIN t024 AS t024_100
   ON
    t024_100~mandt = @LV_mandt_100 AND
     t024_100~ekgrp = ekko_100~ekgrp
LEFT OUTER JOIN ekko AS ekko_700
   ON
       ekko_700~mandt = @LV_mandt_700 AND
       ekko_100~mandt = @LV_mandt_100 AND
       ekko_100~externalreferenceid = ekko_700~ebeln
  LEFT OUTER JOIN ekpo AS ekpo_100
     ON
       ekko_100~mandt = @LV_mandt_100 AND
       ekpo_100~mandt = @LV_mandt_100 AND
       ekko_100~ebeln = ekpo_100~ebeln
  CLIENT SPECIFIED
FIELDS
  ekko_100~ebeln AS ebeln_100,
  ekko_100~ekgrp AS ekgrp_100,
  t024_100~eknam AS eknam_100,
  ekko_700~ebeln AS ebeln_700,
  ekpo_100~ebelp AS ebelp_100,
  ekpo_100~konnr AS ekpo_100_konnr,
  ekpo_100~txz01 AS ekpo_100_txz01
WHERE
  ekko_100~mandt      = @LV_mandt_100
ORDER BY
  ekko_100~ebeln,
  ekpo_100~ebelp
INTO TABLE @DATA(mt_output)
BYPASSING BUFFER.

 

If I replace the CLIENT SPECIFIED with USING ALL CLIENTS I get an error, that I am not allowed to use field "MANDT" in an ON-constraint.

lv_mandt_100 = '100'.
lv_mandt_700 = '700'.

SELECT
FROM ekko AS ekko_100
LEFT OUTER JOIN t024 AS t024_100
   ON
     t024_100~mandt = @LV_mandt_100 AND
     t024_100~ekgrp = ekko_100~ekgrp
LEFT OUTER JOIN ekko AS ekko_700
   ON
       ekko_700~mandt = @LV_mandt_700 AND
       ekko_100~mandt = @LV_mandt_100 AND
       ekko_100~externalreferenceid = ekko_700~ebeln

  LEFT OUTER JOIN ekpo AS ekpo_100
     ON
       ekko_100~mandt = @LV_mandt_100 AND
       ekpo_100~mandt = @LV_mandt_100 AND
       ekko_100~ebeln = ekpo_100~ebeln
  USING ALL CLIENTS "<- instead of CLIENT SPECIFIED belongig to the warning
FIELDS
  ekko_100~ebeln AS ebeln_100,
  ekko_100~ekgrp AS ekgrp_100,
  t024_100~eknam AS eknam_100,
  ekko_700~ebeln AS ebeln_700,
  ekpo_100~ebelp AS ebelp_100,
  ekpo_100~konnr AS ekpo_100_konnr,
  ekpo_100~txz01 AS ekpo_100_txz01
WHERE
  ekko_100~mandt      = @LV_mandt_100
ORDER BY
  ekko_100~ebeln,
  ekpo_100~ebelp
INTO TABLE @DATA(mt_output)
BYPASSING BUFFER.

Documentation for USING ALL CLIENTS is here: https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/abapselect_client.htm 

Belongig the documentation it's not possible to use USING ALL CLIENTS for a single join. But in the above code I use it for the whole FROM/JOIN.

So, my question is, how should I change the abap code to use USING ALL CLIENTS instead of CLIENT SPECIFIED?

And my other question is, if I use the CLIENT SPECIFIED, will the code in future not function anymore?

Best regards
Lorenzo

entry.title: Re: Unable to Create Cross-Company Code Purchasing across...

entry.title: Re: Download formatted open items list AR/AP excel
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

In the Manage Customer Line Items app within S4 Hana Cloud Public Edition, you can export data to an Excel file using the built-in export functionality.

However, the app does not provide options to customize the format of the exported Excel file directly.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Extending view S1 on fiori app breaks model in extension project
entry.content: You shared both the Component and the manifest but still not the controller. Also, please share code as text and not as image.

entry.title: BADI alternatives for various Sales and Distribution(SD) related Processes/Transactions if any
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

As evident from the subject we are looking for BADI alternatives for various Sales and Distribution(SD) related Processes/Transactions, preferably Cloud compatible. In the nutshell we are seeking guidance on alternatives for SAP-World famous EXITS like MV45AFZZ, MV50AFZ1, RV60AFZZ etc. if any.

I have seen many related questions on this like Sales Order Creation - BAdi for S/4HANA Public Cloud where they are suggesting to use SD_SLS* related BADIs like SD_SLS_MODIFY_HEAD, SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM etc. But these BADIs are Custom Fields and Logic specific meaning they are only for manipulating the fields added using Custom Fields Extensions and hence only those fields are available as CHANGING parameter,

I have tried my level best searching inside the Enhancement Spot ES_SD_SLS_EXTEND for this but couldn't come up with anything fruitful. 

Hence, requesting suggestions here from all gurus on this, or if nothing is available yet does that mean we are still good to use those above mentioned Godfather USER-EXITS 😉 and stick with Tier3 Extensions for such scenarios?

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: Service groups of MDI Product does not appear in /IWFND/V4_ADMIN tcode
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am doing setup instructions for MDI using that https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/d761beaeedd949c18213f449f4617250/32afd67be8dc4e66890171e9924098f6.html?locale=en-US&q=master+data+integration. 
In our scenario we are trying to do replicate product master data S/4HANA to Public Cloud system. So I want to prepare our S/4HANA system as provider. On "Publish OData V4 Service Groups" part there are some services and service groups for MDO and MDI. When I opened the /IWFND/V4_ADMIN tcode, left side of the screen shows us published service groups. I did not see any of the service groups. Then I think I need to publish them. I pressed "Publish Service Groups" button and searched as follows. 

I came across the following pop-up.

Is there anything to do for seeing that service groups in the /IWFND/V4_ADMIN tcode. Or is there a step I'm doing wrong?

Thanks in advance for any input.

Berk.

Can any one please suggest any user exit for this requirement .

Thanking you .

Mohammed

I look forward to hearing from you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Story Report in SuccessFactors- I am trying to get co...
entry.content:
Sorry, but it's really hard to understand why are you strugelling that long with a pretty simple report set-up. We don't have any screenshots from you, therefore it's almost impossible to validate what you are doing and give you a precise advice. Here is an example of how I would do similar report:

Create these 2 measures:

then create a table (I don't have values for weight as it's not in my Goal plan):

entry.title: Re: I would like to know how to obtain SOC1 and SOC2 repo...
entry.content:
Hi,

thank you for the details provided.

Please visit 'SAP Trust Center --> Compliance --> Compliance Finder' related to your request:

- SOC1:

https://www.sap.com/about/trust-center/certification-compliance/compliance-finder.html?sort=latest_desc&search=soc

https://www.sap.com/about/trust-center/certification-compliance/compliance-finder.html?sort=latest_desc&tag=compliance-document:compliance-offering/soc-1-bridge-letter

- SOC2:

https://www.sap.com/about/trust-center/certification-compliance/compliance-finder.html?sort=latest_desc&tag=compliance-document:compliance-offering/soc-2

https://www.sap.com/about/trust-center/certification-compliance/compliance-finder.html?sort=latest_desc&tag=compliance-document:compliance-offering/soc-2-bridge-letter

Here you can search for the SOC1 and SOC2 reports, read its details and act accordingly.

SAP Support  - including component CA-AUD - does not deal with SOC reports or any other compliance-related issues, please contact the Compliance Department (links above) in case of any additional questions.

Thank you and have a nice day,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Hi Guru's Help me on periodic billing plan Item net v...
entry.content:
Business wants  to show item net value and billing plan must be same

Not sure what is the issue currently you have or what configuration is set.  Based on your above comments, I presume, you wanted to see same value as billing plan value and net value in which case, try setting "N" as Calculation type to your pricing condition and test

Sara

Best regards Sascha

Need to understand steps and transaction needs to be executed to accomplish the said task. 

 

Thanks & Regards 

S

entry.title: Re: CDC - Other consent statement (not mandatory) is beco...
entry.content:
Hello @arunaruva,

Let me share my knowledge about this topic. If you still have questions after reading this, please don't hesitate to ask in the comments.

In the Customer Data Cloud, Consents may be:
1) Required (Mandatory) / Not required (Not mandatory)
If Consent is required, it means only true values are acceptable for the user to become fully registered.
If Consent is not required, it means both true and false values are acceptable for the user to become fully registered.
2) Active / Not active
If Consent is active, it means the Consent has to be presented to the end user ().
If Consent is not active, it means the Consents shouldn't be presented to the end user.

Let's review your case: Not required (not mandatory) active Consent.
In your case, the Consent has to be presented to the end user, but may not be selected/accepted (false value is acceptable) to become a fully registered user. But it is still required from a schema point of view, because it's active and CDC expects to get a value (true or false).

When you tried to map the Consent to isConsentGranted and make it not required for the current screen, CDC understood it as there is at least one screen where this Consent may be not presented to the end user. That's why the system automatically tried to change this Consent from Active to Inactive.

Hope the explanation was clear and helped shed some light on it.
Regards,
Oleh.

entry.title: Re: How to Connect CAPM With Integration Suite ?

And when we try it without posting date it's refused with an error com.sap.gateway.core.ip.component.odata.exception.OsciException: Internal Server Error : 500 : HTTP/1.1

Patrik

entry.title: Re: procedure for the BAPI/FM to change the equipment bom...
entry.content: Did you already perform some search on CSAI_BOM_MAINTAIN?

entry.title: Re: reporting on successfactor business rules and the res...
entry.content:
Hi @tendisachamis 

Please check this KBA -2778212 - "Assigned" field is empty in Configure Business Rules page

As detailed there, if the rule is under basic scenario, the assigned field will be empty (with no green tick), even though the rule is actually assigned. The assignment is only shown for rules under application-specific scenarios.

Hope this helps.


Iulia

entry.title: New Expert-Guided Implementation: Exploring SAP SuccessFactors HCM Generative AI
entry.content:
"The trainer was knowledgeable and had an excellent understanding of the subject. Very patient and willing to interact with attendees." 

"It was great to have support while working through the tasks. I got a lot more out of it than just watching a presentation." 

These are just some of the experiences shared by participants in our Expert-guided Implementation (EGI) sessions. Now, you have the opportunity to experience it for yourself with our latest offering: "All You Need to Know about SAP SuccessFactors HCM Generative AI." 

This hands-on, two-day remote workshop is designed to help you unlock the full potential of Generative AI within SAP SuccessFactors. And the best part? SAP Enterprise Support Academy is included with every SAP cloud subscription, providing valuable learning resources at no additional cost. 

What is an EGI? 

An EGI is an interactive, multi-day training that combines expert-led instruction with practical application. Here’s how it works: 

  • An SAP expert guides you through key tasks step by step. 
  • You apply what you've learned in your own SAP environment, with the expert available for support. 

This structured approach ensures you gain both theoretical knowledge and hands-on experience, helping you build confidence in using SAP solutions effectively. 

Prerequisites 

To participate in this session, ensure the following prerequisites are met: 

  • Your tenant is located at the supported data centers as per SuccessFactors Generative AI Availability. 
  • You have completed the purchase and provisioning for the necessary SKU (8016532) and AI Units, and have signed the Terms & Conditions. 
  • You have already implemented the corresponding modules for the Premium AI use cases you intend to activate. 

Agenda 

Topics covered include: 

  • Understanding the benefits and capabilities of Generative AI in SAP SuccessFactors.

  • Validating requirements and ensuring customer readiness for Premium AI Activation.

  • Walking through practical use cases, capabilities, and setup steps.

  • Tips for monitoring AI Unit consumption and troubleshooting common issues (e.g., permission errors).

  • Guided steps to enabling Generative AI in your demo instances for the following use cases:

    • AI-Assisted Writing for 360 Reviews
    • AI-Assisted Goals
    • AI-Assisted Job Descriptions in Recruiting
    • AI-Assisted Skills Matcher
    • AI-Assisted Job Recommendations (CSB)
    • AI-Assisted Interview Insights (Prerequisite: Teams App for SAP)
    • AI-Assisted Career Insights
    • AI-Assisted Person Insights for Compensation Discussions

Download the Summary Document for this session.

How to Register 

Check the latest schedule and secure your spot: 

  • Upcoming session: 
Day 1 : Monday 28/04/202511:00 AM - 01:00 PM (New York)
Day 2 : Tuesday 29/04/202511:00 AM - 01:00 PM (New York)
  • Registration closes one week before the session date - sign up early! 
  • Sessions require a minimum number of participants to take place. 
  • Once registered, you'll receive a confirmation email with dial-in details sent to your S-User email. 

Register Now

This Expert-Guided Implementation (EGI) is brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy. Before you can access the registration links, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.** 

 Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps 

Beyond EGI sessions, SAP Enterprise Support offers additional resources to help you drive innovation. SAP Enterprise Support value maps provide expert-led resources, tailored recommendations, and advisory services - all included in your SAP cloud subscription. Get started today: 

  • One-time sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, using a valid S-User ID (please allow up to 2 hours for processing) 
  • Access SAP Enterprise Support value maps for personalized guidance 
  • Request a call with our solution area experts for tailored advice 

Value Maps are part of SAP Enterprise Support’s commitment to your success. Visit the SAP Enterprise Support value maps landing page to explore our range of topics and start engaging with us today. 

 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 

About the author: 

  • Alejandro Azcarate is a Global Topic Owner for SAP Enterprise Support value maps, specializing in SAP SuccessFactors HCM. Based in Madrid, Spain, Alejandro has been with SAP since 2007, holding various roles across the organization. Today, he focuses on helping businesses navigate HR transformation through SAP Enterprise Support offerings.  

Have questions, comments or feedback? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact  
@alejandroazca

entry.title: Re: CREATE_DEEP_ENTITY using RAP ( Unmanaged )
entry.content:
Hello @Tisha_Haria and @Ramya_Priya,

For the deep create method there are three returned entities,

to get the response in case of success use the mapped entity as follow:

wa_order_ord = VALUE #(
                                %key                  =  <fs>-%key
                                production_order      =  <fs>-%key
                                ).

APPEND wa_order_ord TO mapped-_order.

        loop at it_result assigning FIELD-SYMBOL(<hu_s>).

          wa_order_ser-hu = <hu_s>-hu.
          APPEND wa_order_ser TO mapped-_serial.
        endloop.

if the issue is in accessing the components themselves then its in the blog's code where the header and items are assigned separately then handled as needed :

    DATA(lv_import) = order.     " Header entity
    DATA(lv_import_sr) = serial. " Item entity
    LOOP AT lv_import_sr ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<sr>) .
          LOOP AT <sr>-%target ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<tr>).

            wa_order_ser = VALUE #(
                         %key-production_order =   lv_ord_no
                         %key-serial           =   <tr>-serial
                         production_order      =   lv_ord_no
          ).

            wa_report_ser = VALUE #(
                          %key-production_order =   lv_ord_no
                          %key-serial           =   <tr>-serial
                          production_order      =   lv_ord_no
            ).

            wa_fail_ser = VALUE #(
                        %key-production_order =   lv_ord_no
                        %key-serial           =   <tr>-serial
                        production_order      =   lv_ord_no
             ).


            "insert code for items handling
            

          ENDLOOP.


        ENDLOOP.

Please let me know if that covers the issue you are facing or if further details are needed

entry.title: How to make Maximum Stacking Factor field visible in the material master in SAP ECC?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Greetings!

I need help for enabling the Maximum stacking factor (MARM-MAX_STACK) field in the material master in ECC. I have already checked the relevant field selection group 135 in which all the field references are set as 'optional entry'  (except for MM03 and MMVD which are set to 'display'). However, the the max. stacking factor is invisible in the material master. So, what is the way to make it visible?

Thank you

entry.title: Automatic Sales Price Calculation using Pricing Worklist in S/4HANA Retail
entry.content:
Business Scenario:

Retail clients heavily rely on the selling price of articles as a key factor in their business success. Various elements, including article cost, profit margins, and market conditions, influence the final selling price, therefore accurate calculation of the selling price considering all the key factors is crucial for maintaining competitiveness, ensuring profitability, and meeting customer expectations.

Problem Statement:

The purchase price is a crucial factor in determining the final selling price, but it often fluctuates due to varying supplier agreements and market conditions. Keeping track of these frequent changes and promptly adjusting the sales price accordingly can be challenging. Ensuring that final selling price reflects the latest purchase costs requires a systematic approach which can automatically consider such changes to accurately calculate new selling price and remain competitive in the market.

Solution:

If the purchase price or the parameters for source determination changes, it is advisable to check the existing sales prices with respect to the new margin and perform a new sales price calculation. The pricing worklist is used to perform new calculations for certain articles and the relevant organizational levels, this is only possible if their sales price calculation(s) is (are) affected by pricing-relevant changes. This is flagged by a change pointer or a document index.

Pricing Worklist is a functionality used to manage and streamline the pricing process by tracking and processing price changes efficiently. It helps retailers maintain up-to-date sales prices by capturing changes triggered by factors such as purchase price fluctuations and automatically determining all previous calculations affected by the changes. This enables you to perform new sales price calculations considering all the changes.

Pre-requisite, Configuration and Step by Step process to use Pricing worklist for Sales Price Calculation in S/4HANA

Sales prices are generally calculated on the basis of the purchase price, which is in turn calculated using the purchase price calculation schema on the basis of condition types affecting pricing (the basic purchase price, vendor discount, for example). Other pricing-relevant parameters including the regular vendor  indicator in the purchase info record and the source of supply indicator in the article master data are evaluated in supply source determination.

If you want to use the pricing worklist function, you must make sure that when you save a sales price a pricing document is created with a document index. This setting is made under Pricing type.

  • Pricing documents with adjustments for changes in conditions:

    This document index determines only those calculations affected by a change in purchase price.

  • Pricing documents with adjustments for changes in conditions and master data:

    With this document index, calculations that are affected by changes to the source of supply parameters are entered, in addition to those affected by changes in purchase prices.

The entries in tables S111 and WIND are controlled using filters. These filters are Customizing table.

Prerequisite: 

The prerequisite for creating change pointers for the pricing worklist is that there’s an initial pricing document (table WKBP) in the system for a new article, because the last pricing document is used to determine the calculation settings (e.g. purchase- and sales price determination sequence) when the articles are re-calculated using the pricing worklist function.

Configuration:

1. First, you activate the direct entry for creating the worklist for document category 10 (Pricing: Pricing worklist). IMG: → Logistics General → Retail Pricing → Pricing Worklist → Direct Entry for Creating Worklist → Activation of the Direct Entry for Creating the Worklist

(You can also use Program: RMEBEMIG to set direct entry indicator for Pricing)

2. Then, table T6I2A determines the scope of changes for direct entry into the worklist: IMG: → Logistics General → Retail Pricing → Pricing Worklist → Direct Entry for Creating Worklist → Define the Scope of Change for Direct Entry in Worklist.

3. Table T6I1 contains the condition types and corresponding condition tables that are to be checked for changes: IMG: → Logistics General → Retail Pricing → Pricing Worklist → Control Automatic Document Adjustment → Control Pricing Document Adjustment for External / Internal Procurement.

So this means that the system creates the entries for condition-related changes, which are relevant for the pricing worklist, in the WIND — Document Index: Conditions for Automatic Document Adjustment table.

The pricing worklist is used to create a list of newly performed calculations in the form of pricing documents. This includes all calculations affected by the changes and their validity periods. A comparison is made between the entries in table S111 and those in table WIND via field VARKEY.

Procedure:

1. Create a buffer of data out of the changed parameters (known simply as a "worklist") which forms the basis for generating the pricing worklist.

You do not have to perform this Customizing activity if you have made the settings for the direct entries in the worklist for condition changes in Retail Pricing (document category 10) under Logistics - General  Retail Basic Data Activation of the Direct Entry for Creating the Worklist.

2. Generate the pricing worklist from the worklist of price calculations you wish to update. (T-code: WVN0)

3. When a change is made to the master data concerned in the pricing worklist function (regular vendor indicator and vendor subrange in the info record, source of supply indicator in the article master), you no longer have to perform IMG activities 1 and 2. This is because when the master data change is saved, the pricing that is affected by this is determined at the same time and the appropriate entries are created in the pricing worklist.

4. Process the pricing worklist for specific articles and organizational units. (T-code: WVN1/WVN2)

5. The new prices that are calculated are stored, as before, in pricing documents. You can change the results by setting certain parameters, such as the article markup rate, the margin, the sales price or the final price.

6. Save new sales prices that you are happy with and delete any price calculations that you do not want.

2.Any new Endpoints, I need to add? Existing one is present (screenshot 2 ).

entry.title: Re: Advanced Return Management / Scrapping with movement...
entry.content:
Copying movement type 551 or creating a new movement type involves lot of configuration and testing. Instead, what you can do is to create a variant in TVARVC table with the required field values (field values can be Sales Org, Delivery type, Shipping Point, Ship-to etc.,) which is specific to that follow up activity. Now with the delivery user exit, set a validation in such a way that as and when the delivery is created, compare the fields in TVARVC with LIKP and if matches, blank the reason code field.
By implementing this logic, any time, you can change the field values without ABAP help or TR movement

Vikas.T

entry.content:
Dear Amit,

Excellent blog.

entry.title: How to Add Custom Fields to Sales Order Application with Custom Fields
entry.content:
Hi Community,

In this blog post, I want to explain step-by-step how to add new fields to the sales order screens and the related user fields table in SAP using the "Custom Fields" application, and how to manage these fields.

We are going to the Custom Fields application.

 
 
As shown in the image, we click the "Create" button. Then, on the next screen, we select the "Business Context," enter the field name, and choose the field type. After that, we follow the steps below.

Activation may take a few minutes, depending on the system.
After publishing is complete, you can go to the Sales Orders application and check our field.

I hope that helps you. And If you know an alternative solution, please add it to comments.

Thanks for reading.

Kind Regards.

The planned or preview information is only intended to outline future product direction, and is not a commitment, promise or legal obligation (see https://help.sap.com/docs/disclaimer).

entry.title: Re: Individual purchase requisitons from a single sales o...
entry.content: I need SAP to generate a separate purchase requisition (PR) document number for each individual sales order line, rather than a single PR containing multiple line items. Each sales order line should correspond to a unique PR to ensure separate processing and tracking. How can this be configured?

entry.title: Re: Best Approach for Scheduled Trigger with User Credent...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can use Windows Credential Manager for this.

  • Create a generic credential in Credential Manager.
  • Call that credential in BPA using the "Get Local Agent Credentials" activity.
  • This credential can be updated using the "Set Local Agent Credentials" activity. For this, you can create a form trigger process that the user will trigger to update the credential on your VM/local machine where the unattended agent is running.
  • To notify the user, you can create a simple automation scheduled to run at the start of the day. This automation can send an email containing a form link in the body using the "Send Email" activity.

Regards,

Manish

• Every 12-months of employment after the 6-months

entry.title: Re: Need Validation to Prevent Output Trigger When No Rel...
entry.content:
Hi @RazzaqAbdul 
As you mentioned - Release strategy criteria is based on "purchasing group, document type, order net value".
my suggestion would be - Keep/allocate all your purchasing groups (including standard ones) in your release strategy. then we may avoid the Issue.

If you are specific about the purchasing groups - Provide the list to technical team along with other parameters like document type. then the requirement will be 'If user enters other than the required purchasing groups, PO should throw an error'. 
(In future, If any new purch. grp requirement comes we need to update the above list as well)

entry.title: Re: SAP Utilities Roadmap 2025 Onwards
entry.content:
Thank You Pieter for quick response.

I have a specific German customer requirement that they want to call a existing RFC , BAPI or Odata Services(POST call) from external world and SAP ISU creates the ISU Technical Master Data (Connection obj, Premise, Installations, Premise, Device Location etc ) and returns all the numbers back to calling API. 
I am checking if there are some existing POST APIs, RFC, BAPIs available anywhere in any form which could be used directly (without any custom development) for this purpose or SAP is planning anything similar in future releases.
Could you share some highlights on such a scenario and SAP's strategy , pros, cons. I will be thankful to you.  
Thanks in advance!!
Regards,
Amit

entry.title: Re: Creating Training Guides in SAP
entry.content:
I'm not familiar with SAP ERP PRD for warehouse use. However, I reviewed your documentation, and a pic/snippet is worth a thousand words. 

I commend you for taking the time to develop a training guide. It takes time on the front end to draft the documentation, but the payoff is enormous. It allows for consistency across teams, allows new hires to get up to speed quickly, and we all have those moments when we do something infrequently and can't remember. Having up to date reference material improves productivity and allows employees to be self-reliant.

entry.title: Re: Add a second static PDF document to Customer Statemen...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

To add a second static PDF document to the Customer Statement Correspondence app in S4 Hana Cloud Public Edition, you can follow theses steps :

1. Customize the Form Template

Access the Form Template : navigate to the Maintain Form templates app

Download the Template : locate and download the existing form template associated with customer statements

Edit with Adobe Livecycle Designer : open the downloaded template using the mentioned software. Here you can integrate the second static PDF by embedding it directly into the form or by adding a subform that references the additional document

Upload the Modified Template : after making the necessary adjustements, upload the updated template back into the Maintain Form Templates app

 

2. Configure Output Parameters :

Access Output Parameter Determination : use the Output Parameter Determination app to define the conditions under which the modified form template should be utilized

Set Determination Rules : establish rules that specify when the system should use the updated template containing the second PDF

 

3. Schedule and Generate Customer Statements : 

Schedule jobs : Use the Schedule Accounts Receivable Jobs app to set up periodic jobs for generating and sending customer statements. Ensure that the job parameters align with the conditions set in the output parameter determination

Review Correspondence History : after execution, you can review the correspondence history to verify that the statements, now including the second static PDF document, have been generated and sent appropriately.

 

I hope this will help you answer your questions :).

Best regards,

Jeremy

the reported error message is documented in attached SAP note 3287549. Fix for the issue has been released in version 10.0H FP2305

Kind Regards

Thank you for looking into this.

entry.title: 5 Essentials Every Leader Needs for a Future-Proof Software Strategy
entry.content:
"The pace of change has never been this fast, yet it will never be this slow again." – Justin Trudeau 

Modern business success requires more than stellar products or services to win the market. Leading business success requires companies to adapt both rapidly and on a large scale while ensuring a seamless transition. Businesses now rely on software to drive their operations because speed functions as the main business currency.  

Several organizations maintain the philosophy of handling software as a tool for implementation and updating before moving on. The reality is different. The business world depends on software as a living system which drives organizational momentum. 

The Changing Landscape of Software Development 

  • AI is revolutionizing software development. Modern startups manage to deliver their products within weeks despite traditional industry timelines which span months. 
  • Customer expectations have skyrocketed. Established brands implement software solutions to forecast upcoming customer wants and tailor their services before needs materialize.  

Your software strategy needs to follow current realities instead of the conventional methods in place. The current strategy proves ineffective therefore you should reconsider your approach. Here’s how. 

Build for Resilience, Not Just Efficiency 

“Scaling up is easy, but scaling smart is where most businesses fail.” 

Every business in the market seeks operational efficiency combined with reduced expenses. A software strategy exclusively built for efficiency will not survive future disruptions.  

When it comes to business software resilience is about both coping with busy times along with minimizing outages and also about enabling your organization to evolve smoothly beyond current needs. 

  • Companies leveraging microservices and event-driven architectures report 72% faster product development with lower downtime. 
  • Their approach goes beyond immediate requirements by engineering solutions designed for future scalability and adaptability. 

Key Takeaway: The design of software systems should include adaptability and scalability technology which supports lasting corporate expansion. 

AI and Automation: A Competitive Edge, Not a Trend 

“AI won’t replace developers, but companies that leverage AI will outpace those that don’t.” 

AI has graduated from being a buzzword to become an essential business-driving advantage that organizations must use. The implementation of AI throughout organizational operations helps organizations achieve major marketplace success. 

  • AI-powered coding assistants reduce development time by 40%. 
  • Automated testing and DevOps pipelines accelerate releases while minimizing human error. 
  • AI-driven analytics enable real-time, data-driven decision-making. 

The true game-changer? Predictive software engineering, where AI anticipates failures, suggests fixes, and adapts in real time. 

Key Takeaway: Organizations achieving superior performance through AI tend to view it as their fundamental business operation rather than an accessory. 

Security: A Business Imperative, Not an IT Checklist 

“Customer trust is your strongest muscle in a digital world.” 

Security failures don’t just cost money—they damage reputation, trust, and competitive standing. Security has become a critical boardroom topic because cybercrime costs will surpass $10.5 trillion annually. 

The best approach? 

  • Organizations should implement Zero Trust architecture for achieving rigorous access control management. 
  • Security implementation must be an essential component that runs from development start to finish using DevSecOps methodologies. 
  • AI-driven threat intelligence to predict and neutralize cyber risks before they escalate. 

A majority of 60% of organizations has integrated security measures into every developmental phase which has led to better initial vulnerability management. 

Key Takeaway: The integration of security principles needs to appear at the beginning of your software strategy instead of being treated as a final addition. 

Prioritize Cross-Functional Collaboration 

“Software fails when business and engineering teams speak different languages.” 

Too often, software development happens in silos: 

  • IT builds the software. 
  • Business teams request changes. 
  • Product teams mediate. 

This disconnect leads to delays, misalignment, and software that fails to drive real business outcomes. The most successful companies take a different approach: 

  • Product, engineering, and business teams work as a single, aligned unit. 
  • Decision-making cycles are streamlined, reducing time-to-market by up to 30%. 
  • Cross-functional collaboration ensures software is both functional and strategically valuable. 

Key Takeaway: Software must be integrated with business strategy—not treated as a standalone function. 

Embrace Agility Over Perfection 

“The biggest risk isn’t moving too fast. It’s standing still.” 

Organizations find themselves performing excessive planning which aims to create perfect software solutions. While competitors push their products to market quickly they then both improve and achieve market leadership in simultaneous release cycles. 

What works instead? 

  • Speed beats perfection. Iteration is more valuable than getting everything right the first time. 
  • Agile, DevOps, and continuous deployment aren’t just methodologies—they’re survival strategies. 
  • The market doesn’t wait; neither should your software. 

The companies that adopt quick cycle development with practical user insights constantly succeed above organizations which maintain their traditional paradigm. 

Key Takeaway: The best software strategies prioritize adaptability and iteration over rigid, long-term planning. 

Are You Leading the Future or Just Keeping Up? 

Innovation isn’t slowing down—it’s accelerating. Successful companies will not be formed by organizations that merely adopt technological developments. These leading organizations will build their software strategies based on adaptability as well as AI and security and collaboration and agility. 

So, ask yourself: 

  • Is your software strategy truly future-ready? 
  • Or are you just keeping up while others surge ahead? 

Because in today’s landscape, you don’t just need great software—you need software that keeps you ahead of the game.

Whether you've just graduated or have a few years of experience, we welcome you to shape the future with SAP. Explore world-class graduate programs or apply for entry-level jobs Graduate jobs
There you also can find PhD student offerings.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Missing Business Context "Appl Interface WBSElement Details (PS_WBSELEMENT)" in public cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We had a requirement to extend standard cds view I_WBSElementData_2 .

For that i have explored the details about this cds in api.sap.com and got to know we can extend this cds using custom field, with business context "Appl Interface WBSElement Details (PS_WBSELEMENT)" .

But I am not getting above business context in my "custom field" app. Sharing the screenshots for reference.

Do we have any other ways to extend this cds view. Please let me know your view on this?

Regards,

Jagadhatri

ABAP Extensibility SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hi @Eswar14 

To get and stay certified: 

SAP Learning Hub subscription 

  • Access to expert-led live sessions, on-demand practice systems, enhanced analytics, four certification exam attempts, and short, non-proctored assessments to stay certified. 
  • Exclusive solution that provides everything you need to prepare for, take, and maintain your SAP Certifications, with one single subscription.  
  • Annual subscription cost per user. Available directly on SAP Store or by contacting your SAP representative.  
  • SAP PartnerEdge members can learn more about and subscribe to SAP Learning Hub, partner edition.  

 

To get certified: 

  1. SAP Certification exam, one attempt or SAP Certification exam, six attempts: Includes one or six exam attempts that you can purchase to get certified. These offerings do not include access to assessments to stay certified. 
  2. SAP Certification exam, two attempts: 
    • Includes two exam attempts, plus 10 hours of hands-on practice system access. Does not include assessments to stay certified. 
    • You can purchase on the SAP Store.  

  3. Staying certified 
    The simplest path to staying certified is through an SAP Learning Hub subscription, which includes yearly assessments and preparation content to stay certified. These assessments are short, non-proctored quizzes with unlimited attempts, and must be successfully completed before your certification expiry date. Alternatively, if you don’t have a subscription to SAP Learning Hub, you will need to retake the certification exam(s) every year. 

Please check our SAP Certification support site where you find a wealth of resources including how-to guides, troubleshooting tips, and detailed FAQs about SAP Certification, all designed to help you quickly. If you can't find the answer here, create a case - our team will be happy to help you..

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: SAP Service and Asset Manager Gis Enhancement
entry.content:
Hi, The map can show any location which has GIS cordinates attached to it so you need to make sure each functional location, equipment and order have the GIS cordinates. If all of them have the same GIS cordinates, then you will only see one. We map the cordinates based on this order,

Work Order -> Equipment -> Functional Location -> Notification -> Routes -> Stops.

 

So if you have a functional location with 5 equipments and all of the equipments have the same cordinates as functional location then you may only see functional location.

You can change this order from the metadata and it's customizable

entry.title: Re: FSM HTML Report Font-size Page Counter // error only...
entry.content: Did you get this issue solved?

entry.title: Urgent Issue: Cheque Printing and Assignment Field Greyed Out in Outgoing Payments
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We are facing a critical issue related to cheque printing while processing outgoing payments. Our requirement is to print a cheque when executing a payment. However, when using the Post Outgoing Payment app, the system does not generate a cheque.

To work around this limitation, we attempted to use Create Single Payment while selecting an invoice. However, we encountered the following issues:

  1. Assignment Field Greyed Out

    • We need to enter the cheque number in the assignment field to enable automatic clearing in the manual bank statement. Since this field is greyed out, we are unable to enter the cheque number, preventing automatic document matching.

  2. Multiple Bank Accounts Against a Single House Bank

    • Our business scenario involves multiple bank accounts under a single house bank. However, the Bank Account field is also greyed out in the payment process, making it unclear how we can manage payments across different bank accounts.

These issues are significantly impacting our cheque payment process. We request your immediate assistance in resolving them.

Expected Resolution:

  • Enable the Assignment field to allow manual entry of the cheque number for seamless bank statement reconciliation.

  • Provide a solution for managing multiple bank accounts under a single house bank within the payment process.

This is a critical business requirement, and we would appreciate a prompt resolution. Please provide guidance or a workaround at the earliest.

Looking forward to your urgent response.

entry.title: Inactive Items Not Displayed in User Learning History When Using Search Bar
entry.content:
I’ve noticed that inactive learning items are not appearing in the user’s Learning History when searched using the search bar. However, they do appear in the full list view. Interestingly, this behavior differs between instances—inactive items are visible in the Test instance but not in Production.

Does anyone know where I can find the relevant setting to ensure inactive items are also displayed in the Production instance when using the search bar?

Thanks!

entry.title: Generating OAuth Client in SAC, how do we make our tenant to specify Client Id and Client Secret
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

In our SAP Analytics Cloud tenant, we see only option to generate automatically Client Id and Client Secret while creating OAuth Client. And we are able to generate the access token for API usage.

But we observed for one of our customer, having their SAC application, where they can manually specify the Client ID and Client Secret, because of this reason, token is not being generated and getting the error "

Unexpected response code received: 401. Details:
{"error":"unauthorized_client"}

I basically wanted to know, token generation functionality varies based generating client id and secret Vs manually specifying client id and secret ?  Or do we need to follow any rules while manually specifying client id and client secret ? 

Any help on this regard would be really helpful.

Thanks,

Sreedhar Rusuma

entry.title: Re: ML_GET_BLOCKED_INFO

Guess I'll need an actual DBA to try and run a trace to see what's calling ML_GET_BLOCKED_INFO, using the wrong number of arguments; maybe? I will say it is very aggravating when hitting this error during synchronization operations... Having to wait several minutes for it to release the worker thread(s). Regardless, a solution to this issue still alludes me. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Custom CDS Based search models with Long Text support in S/4HANA on-premise
entry.content:
@Philip Have you managed to successfully implement a CDS-based search model?

I keep getting errors, like "'@UI' must be annotated (at header and elements) [Enterprise Search]", even though I follow all the recommendations and have annotated my fields accordingly. (I stripped everything down to two fields to make sure there is nothing else it could be complaining about).

entry.title: Re: Individual purchase requisitons from a single sales o...
entry.content:
• Go to STVARV
  • Create a variable name 
  • Under Selection Options, have combination of fields like sales org, order type, schedule line category

Now, add the logic in USEREXIT_MOVE_FIELD_TO_ME_REQ in such a way that when an entry is created in VBAP or VBEP, each POSNR from VBAP to create a separate PR. 

By this approach, you are not hardcoding the logic in user exit where no need to depend on ABAP team to modify in case of Business requirement changes.  Super users can themselves change the field names or delete the entry if not required.

entry.title: BP Roles for InHouse Banking
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
As a part of In-House Bank set up, we are trying to add the BP roles IHB100 and IHB200 to the Business Partners.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b8e68a90bfff4ff0906d5db5342bdece/1ac485d510db46d1b9a084364b0d1479.html 

However, the SSCUI 103008 - Define BP Roles, doesn't provide a provision to add new BP Roles. Thus, could you kindly suggest any way on how these roles can be added first to the SSCUI, so that we can then add these roles to the Business Partners required for In-House Banking?

Could some scope item activation add these BP roles to the SSCUI automatically? If yes, which scope item?
Thanks in advance

entry.title: Set nobinpt via bdc_transaction (SAP standard form for bdc)?
entry.content:
I want to set nobinpt so that it does not get set as X when the BDC report is getting called. Currently, we are using bdc_transaction because we are also skipping the initial screen of PA30. Now, in this, we cannot set nobinpt so, I tried to set this via ctu_params and use the following instead of bdc_trasaction. 

CALL TRANSACTION ‘PA30’ USING bdc_data OPTIONS opt MESSAGES INTO mess tab.

now, the issue is that binpt is getting properly set, but the initial screen is not getting skipped, even though I have mentioned it properly in bdcdata.

Any suggestions on how to achieve this would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: SAP Process Orchestration 7.5 - Large Message Handling
entry.content:
Hello Remyak,

Check with your Basis Team to increase server nodes it will process the message are fast.

entry.title: Adobe Document Services future Strategy
entry.content:
We know that ADS on-premise support will be ended at 2027.

What alternatives does SAP offer for on-premise ADS?
Will there be a new on-premise system to replace ADS, or will only cloud-based solutions be available?

We found a link about on-premise dedicator : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/maintenance-strategy-adobe-forms-on-premise/ba-p/13627957
"As announced at the DSAG Technology Days 2024 in Hamburg, SAP HANA Extended Application Services, Advanced Model (XSA) will be the successor platform for Adobe Document Services."
Could you verify this statement for this is valid in currently?

"SAP plans to deliver this option during the support time frame of the S/4HANA 2025 release."
Could you send me more details and information about it? For example S/4HANA 2025 will containe the XSA with ADS functionality? How can we imagine the new on-premise architecture?

Thanks in advance
